label: GDS
citation: Brink TL, Yesavage JA, Lum O, et al. (1982). Screening tests for geriatric depression. Clin Gerontol. 1(1):37–43. https://dx.doi.org/10.1016/0022-3956(82)90033-4
license: Public domain. Available free of charge for clinical and research use at Stanford University.
description: A 30-item (or 15-item short form) yes/no self-report scale for depression in older adults. Designed to be simple to administer and minimize somatic items that can confound depression ratings in elderly patients.
scoring: GDS-30: 0–9 normal; 10–19 mild depression; 20–30 severe depression. GDS-15: 0–4 normal; 5–8 mild; 9–11 moderate; 12–15 severe.
items: Are you basically satisfied with your life? (N)
Have you dropped many of your activities and interests? (Y)
Do you feel that your life is empty? (Y)
Are you in good spirits most of the time? (N)
Are you afraid that something bad is going to happen to you? (Y)
Do you feel happy most of the time? (N)
Do you prefer to stay at home, rather than going out and doing new things? (Y)
Do you feel you have more problems with memory than most? (Y)
Do you think it is wonderful to be alive now? (N)
Do you feel pretty worthless the way you are now? (Y)
Do you feel full of energy? (N)
Do you feel that your situation is hopeless? (Y)
Do you think that most people are better off than you are? (Y)
